manifest.name: Learning System

name: Learning System

releases.author: jady21a
releases.description:
Post-0.2.0 polish release covering the bugs and rough edges reported after the community-compliance refactor.

## Fixes

- **Annotation editor**: typing an annotation and then clicking the note text (or any external area that closes the editor) now reliably saves. The blur path was previously skipping the save when the editor had already been removed from the DOM.
- **Mindmap colors**: nodes, branch lines and the answer-side correctness highlights now use a vivid Catppuccin palette and the Obsidian theme variables (`--background-primary`, `--text-normal`, `--color-red/green`), so the map is readable on both light and dark themes.
- **Review card · Answer details**: the section now renders even when you didn't type anything — each blank shows up as `(empty) → correct answer`, marked wrong.
- **Review card · empty hint bar**: the stray styled bar that appeared between "Fill in the blanks" and the input is gone (was an empty `.cloze-input-hint` div).
- **Stats CSS leak**: the week‑over‑week stat row's `.comparison-item` was overriding the review card's same‑named class with `display: flex; space-between`, stretching the answer-details rows. Renamed to `.stat-comparison-item`.

## Removed

- Level‑up `Notice` popup (`🏆 Lv X xxx!`) — leftover from the dropped gating system. Milestone congratulation notices stay.

## Install

Drop `manifest.json`, `main.js` and `styles.css` into `.obsidian/plugins/learning-system/` and enable the plugin.

Full diff: 0.2.0...0.2.1
releases.name: v0.2.1 — Bug fixes & UI polish

releases.author: jady21a
releases.description:
## Highlights

### Milestones replace level-gating
- All features are usable from the start — no more "you need Lv3 to unlock review" prompts.
- The level system is reframed as a **milestone (achievement) checklist**: thirteen tasks calibrated by effort/frequency (first extract, ×5 each extract type, 10/30/50/150 reviews, 7‑day streak, 21 active days, etc.).
- Reaching a milestone fires a one‑time congratulation notice.
- New command **Learning System: Show milestones** opens the panel on demand (no persistent UI in the sidebar).

### Annotation editor: flicker fixed
- Right‑clicking ⋮ → Annotation when the sidebar wasn't already focused used to need a second click. Root cause: `active-leaf-change` was unconditionally refreshing the list and destroying the card the menu was attached to.
- Fix: refresh only when the selected file actually changed; resolve the live card by `unit.id` at click time; open the editor synchronously and drop the brittle `isOpening` / `isOpeningEditor` boolean locks.

### Community‑plugin compliance
A two‑phase sweep against the Obsidian guidelines and `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd`:
- **49 → 0** `innerHTML` sites — replaced with DOM API (`createEl` / `setText` / `appendText` / `createSpan`).
- **2 → 0** self‑built `document.body.appendChild(modal)` — converted to Obsidian `Modal` subclasses.
- **3 → 0** `document.querySelector('.cm-editor' / '.cm-content' / '.search-container')` and the global non‑removable listeners they attached.
- **2 → 0** runtime `<style>` injections — `mind-elixir` vendor CSS now bundled into `styles.css`.
- All inline `element.style.*` moved to CSS classes (only dynamic widths still use `setCssProps`).
- UI text passed through sentence‑case rule.
- Settings: drop the disallowed "General" heading.
- Stray `console.log` / `console.debug` removed.

**Result:** `npx eslint src/` reports 0 errors and the build is clean.

### Cleanup
- Removed all 9 `tryUseFeature()` call sites and the function itself.
- Dropped the unused `unlockedFeatures: Set<string>` field from `UnlockProgress`; old save files containing it are silently migrated on load.

## Install

Drop \`manifest.json\`, \`main.js\` and \`styles.css\` into \`.obsidian/plugins/learning-system/\` and enable the plugin in Settings → Community Plugins.

Full diff: 0.1.0...0.2.0
releases.name: v0.2.0 — Milestones + community-compliance refactor
